label: ETAP - Arc Flash Result Analyzer / report export (DRAFT captions)
toolVersionRange: ETAP ~12-22 arc-flash results (AC). IMPORTANT: unlike the SKM/EasyPower templates, NO real ETAP export was available - ETAP's help/manual is not publicly text-accessible. The RESULT FIELD SET is verified from official ETAP documents (Arc Flash Analysis tutorial PDF; Arc Flash Analysis Overview brochure; Result Analyzer product page: arcing current Ia, bolted fault Ibf, FCT, incident energy, arc flash boundary, working distance, gap, equipment type, hazard/risk level; 'Automatically Export / Create MS Excel arc flash reports'), but the exact Excel COLUMN CAPTIONS are probable/assumed. Confirm every caption against one real Result Analyzer Excel export before relying on this template.
policyNote: LIABILITY POSTURE: ServiceCycle stores PE-stamped study RESULTS; it never computes incident energy and never asserts PPE categories. ETAP's computed hazard/risk / PPE category columns are deliberately mapped to NOTHING by this template. Unit conversions here are mechanical only (kV to V, s or cycles to ms with a declared 60 Hz assumption, ft or mm to in, J/cm2 to cal/cm2); no engineering derivation is performed.
sourceNotes: ETAP 'Arc Flash Analysis' tutorial PDF (etap.com/docs/default-source/faqs-tutorials/arc-flash-analysis.pdf, fetched 2026-07-03): confirms the result vocabulary - bolted fault current, arc fault clearing time (FCT), incident energy, flash protection boundary, hazard/risk level per NFPA 70E, working distance, gap, equipment type, system grounding - and the AF Result Analyzer workflow.

ETAP 'Arc Flash Analysis Overview' brochure PDF (etap.com/docs/default-source/brochures/arc-flash-analysis-overview.pdf, fetched 2026-07-03): 'Automatically Export / Create MS Excel arc flash reports', metric or English units, results grouped by equipment location.
ETAP Arc Flash Result Analyzer product page (etap.com/arc-flash/arc-flash-result-analyzer, fetched 2026-07-03): Excel export, per-column sorting/filtering, Ia variation checks.

ETAP element naming convention ('ID' identifies every element; kV-based voltages throughout, e.g. 13.8 kV) from the tutorial; 'BusID'/'NomkV' shapes align with the repo's existing DRAFT ETAP DataX header map in lib/arcFlashAfxMultiTable.ts.

BLOCKED sources (noted, not worked around): IOP open-access paper 'Arc flash analysis using ETAP platform' (iopscience.iop.org, PDF and landing page both returned empty to fetch); Northeastern University thesis PDF (repository.library.northeastern.edu, returned empty); raw curl egress from the sandbox (blocked). These would likely show verbatim ETAP result tables.

mappings.source: ETAP identifies every element by ID (tutorial); Result Analyzer rows are per faulted bus/PD/terminal. Exact caption unverified; 'BusID' matches the repo's DRAFT DataX map.
ETAP is kV-based throughout (tutorial examples 13.8 kV); 'NomkV' matches the repo's DRAFT DataX map. Exact caption unverified.
'Ibf' is ETAP/IEEE 1584 notation for bolted fault current in kA (official ETAP doc vocabulary); caption spelling unverified.
'Ia' is ETAP's notation for arcing current in kA (Result Analyzer filters '% Ia' variation; official ETAP doc vocabulary); caption spelling unverified.
ETAP tutorial: 'Arc Fault Clearing Time (FCT)' in seconds; brochure: 'Arc Fault Duration'. Caption spelling unverified.
mappings.note: Mechanical conversion: s x 1000 = ms. FCT is ETAP's total clearing time for the row's clearing device.

mappings.source: ETAP commonly expresses protective-device times in cycles as well as seconds; a cycles-captioned FCT column has not been sighted in an export.
mappings.note: Mechanical conversion with a DECLARED assumption: cycles x (1000 / 60 Hz) = ms. frequencyAssumptionHz in this template is 60 (North American systems). A 50 Hz project must not use this alias.

mappings.source: ETAP tutorial: working distance 'typically 18 inches for low voltage equipment'; English-units export mode. Caption spelling unverified.
ETAP exports support metric units (brochure); metric caption unsighted.
ETAP tutorial: 'gaps between conductors' with IEEE 1584 typical values (mm is the IEEE 1584 unit). Caption spelling unverified.
ETAP tutorial: bus rating page selects the equipment type (MCC, switchgear, open air). Whether/how the export captions it is unverified; 'Type' matches the repo's DRAFT DataX map.
ETAP supports IEEE 1584-2018 electrode configurations (VCB/VCBB/HCB/VOA/HOA per etap.com IEEE 1584-2018 page); export caption unsighted.
cal/cm2 is ETAP's English-units incident-energy unit (official ETAP doc vocabulary); caption spelling unverified.
ETAP exports support metric units (brochure); metric caption unsighted.
mappings.note: Mechanical conversion: J/cm2 / 4.184 = cal/cm2 (thermochemical calorie, the IEEE 1584 convention).

mappings.source: AFB is ETAP's arc-flash-boundary abbreviation (official ETAP doc vocabulary); caption spelling unverified.
ETAP frequently reports AFB in feet in English-units mode (doc vocabulary); caption spelling unverified.
ETAP reports the clearing/source protective device per row (tutorial: labels generated 'for every protective device location'); caption unsighted.
mappings.note: Device NAME/designation, not a catalog model number; mapped to deviceModel for parity with the existing AFX crosswalk.

ignoredByPolicy.note: ETAP computes 'the hazard/risk level according to NFPA 70E' (official tutorial wording) and the Result Analyzer sorts by 'Energy Level' category. Recorded product policy: ServiceCycle never asserts PPE/hazard categories; every tool-computed PPE/hazard-category column is mapped to nothing.

knownUnmapped.note: NFPA 70E shock-protection boundaries; AFX v1 has no shock-boundary fields.
ETAP scenario/bookkeeping and grounding columns with no AFX v1 equivalent. Listed so they are recognized rather than 'unknown'.
